usage: Machine-readable proof ledger for the Gamble Battle matte gothic unit-art workflow. Use this before generation to see which archetypes are proven, which examples were rejected, and which coverage gaps still need a deliberate stress test. Do not treat a proof here as permission to replace live assets; live swaps still require explicit user approval.

style_contract.target: western dark gothic board-game unit art, about 10 percent grounded realism, high-detail matte gothic illustration, Vellum-level dry detail richness, Paisley as secondary contrast context only, powder-matte dry material language, layered fabric/parchment/dry edge wear, hand-painted surface breakup, low-specular ambient light, broad heavy shadow, readable at 96 px
reference_hierarchy: Vellum is the primary and ultimate character style anchor.
Paisley is the secondary contrast anchor for brighter or stranger units that still need the same dry gothic richness.
Creep v5 is a promoted narrow horror anchor for dissociated smooth-alien creepy humanoid/demon/cursed units, not a replacement for Vellum as the global finish anchor.
The Vellum contract token is a small-asset material/detail reference, not the main character palette anchor.
Later accepted/current proofs are narrow coverage examples, not equal global style anchors unless the user explicitly promotes them.
hard_reject_if: sweaty, oily, wet, glossy, plastic, latex, lacquered, or polished-leather skin/clothing
clean fantasy, mobile-game, gacha, cartoon, comic-book, toy-like, or heroic splash-art rendering
low-detail smooth creature model, over-smoothed simplified matte shapes, or palette-only match that lacks Vellum-level dry detail richness
polished bevels, chrome, bright specular highlights, shiny plate, or cinematic bloom
textured/smoky/scenic background instead of flat safety-orange #f84401
style_contract.cutout_default: BiRefNet with --foreground-ml --despill-orange --edge-orange-clean, raw-backed strict audit against exact #f84401 plus border-connected raw orange background-field pixels, and review on checker, black, white, and board scale

primary_anchor.label: Vellum raw
primary_anchor.rule: Ultimate character style reference for mood, material language, detail richness, and side-by-side comparison order. Do not demote or average it with later proofs unless the user explicitly replaces it.

reference_policy.small_asset_rule: The contract token is small-asset material context only. Do not use its warmer parchment palette as a character palette or unit style anchor.
reference_policy.promotion_rule: Later accepted/current proofs are narrow coverage examples only. They do not become global style anchors just because they passed; user must explicitly promote a proof before it can change the anchor hierarchy.
reference_policy.candidate_rule: Current candidates can demonstrate process progress, but they are review-only and never anchor references until approved.
reference_policy.side_by_side_rule: Every serious candidate must be reviewed in a Vellum-first side-by-side comparison before acceptance. Paisley, token, and later accepted proofs can add secondary context, but Vellum remains the first visual question.
reference_policy.veto_rule: Vellum can veto any candidate on dry material finish, detail richness, grounded realism, silhouette mood, and board-scale readability. Later proofs can answer only narrow risk questions and cannot rescue a candidate that is weaker than Vellum.
reference_policy.passing_pool_rule: Do not average the passing pool into the target style. New accepted proofs stay narrow by reference_role unless the user explicitly promotes one into the global anchor hierarchy.
reference_policy.prompt_context_rule: Rows marked with a blocked_* prompt_context_status in candidate style triage must not be used as prompt or style context until they pass fresh Vellum-first visual review or receive explicit user promotion/reclassification.

proofs.proof_goal: Existing narrow mummy/ribbon unit stays readable and matte after gothic refit.
proofs.style_gate: Pass: dry mummy cloth/parchment read, non-glossy, narrow silhouette survives 96 px.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: refined BiRefNet cutout preserved ribbons/body without major orange fringe.
proofs.decision_notes: Accepted as small/narrow plus detached-ribbon proof. Not a live asset replacement.

proofs.proof_goal: Bright contrast unit can keep the goth direction and detached bubble identity without cartoon gloss.
proofs.style_gate: Pass: brighter contrast remains aged, dry, and gothic rather than shiny/cartoon.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: selected BiRefNet cutout was preferred after detached-bubble review.
proofs.decision_notes: Accepted as contrast-side and detached-effects proof. Not a live asset replacement.

proofs.proof_goal: Non-character gameplay token keeps matte wax/parchment/ink language without readable glyphs.
proofs.style_gate: Pass: matte wax/parchment/ink token read at small scale with no shiny jewel treatment.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: selected BiRefNet cutout held the token cleanly.
proofs.decision_notes: Accepted as non-character asset proof.

proofs.proof_goal: Planned horror assassin unit refit, first attempt.
proofs.style_gate: Fail: identity became ripped-apart corpse/dead-flesh horror and still did not match Paisley/Vellum matte gothic finish.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: tendrils survived the refined BiRefNet cutout, but the raw was rejected.
proofs.failure_reason: Lost original smooth oval alien face and smooth gray-blue alien skin; leaned into exposed anatomy, corpse texture, and wet/dead-flesh horror.
proofs.decision_notes: Kept as negative example. It hardened the Creep smooth-alien lock and anti-corpse prompt guard.

proofs.proof_goal: Restore original smooth alien face/skin while pushing Creep into the same matte gothic family as Vellum and Paisley.
proofs.style_gate: Fail: smooth alien identity improved, but the material finish still read too shiny/creature-concept compared with Vellum and Paisley.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: refined BiRefNet cutout preserved black tendril/blade silhouette and board-scale ring.
proofs.failure_reason: Solved more of the original smooth alien face/skin problem than the first rejected proof, but still carried slick head/body highlights and did not lock into the hard matte gothic finish.
proofs.decision_notes: Kept as a superseded negative example. It proves that Creep identity restoration alone is not enough without the hard matte material gate.

proofs.proof_goal: Restore original smooth alien face/skin and force Creep into the same hard matte gothic finish as Vellum and Paisley.
proofs.style_gate: Fail after human review: preserves more of the smooth alien face/skin but still reads too shiny/creature-concept beside Vellum and Paisley.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: refined BiRefNet foreground-ML/despill cutout preserved the tendril ring without visible orange fringe in the review and board preview.
proofs.failure_reason: The body/head and serrated tendrils still carried slick creature-concept highlights and anatomical surface modeling; identity restoration was not enough to meet the matte gothic non-shiny gate.
proofs.decision_notes: Kept as a superseded negative example. It proves Creep needs smooth alien skin plus flat powder-matte material and dull ink tendrils, not just a less wet creature render.

proofs.proof_goal: Restore Creep's original smooth alien skin/face while matching Paisley and Vellum's matte gothic non-shiny finish more strictly.
proofs.style_gate: Fail after style-drift audit: restores smooth alien face/skin and reduces sweaty corpse shine, but overcorrects into an under-detailed smooth creature that lacks Vellum-level dry gothic illustration richness.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: refined BiRefNet foreground-ML/despill cutout preserves the smooth head/body read, hands/feet, dull tendril ring, and 96 px board-scale silhouette on checker, black, and white.
proofs.failure_reason: Clean/readable but too simplified: the smooth body lost the layered dry texture, tactile surface breakup, small gothic accents, and hand-painted material richness that make Vellum and Paisley work. Detail score was also visibly and metrically far below the reference pair.
proofs.decision_notes: Kept as a negative example for the new audit rule: de-shining must preserve high-detail dry rendering, not smooth the unit into a low-detail creature model. Do not replace assets/units/creep.png.

proofs.proof_goal: Retest Creep after the Vellum-primary audit by preserving smooth alien identity while restoring Vellum-level dry gothic detail richness.
proofs.style_gate: Current candidate after Vellum-primary side-by-side correction: smooth oval alien face and gray-blue skin remain, while dry occult markings, subtle chalk/mottled surface breakup, cord/ribbon/talisman scraps, and dull ink-black serrated tendrils restore more anchor-level detail richness without returning to wet corpse anatomy. Vellum-first audit shows edge/detail proxy 32.65, back in the Vellum/Paisley range rather than the failed smooth Creep's 14.49.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: refined BiRefNet foreground-ML/despill cutout completed, board preview reads on checker/black/white, and Vellum-first audit sheet keeps Creep readable at board scale without making the raw under-detailed.
proofs.decision_notes: Current Creep candidate and first real test of the tightened Vellum-primary process. It is not a live replacement and must not touch assets/units/creep.png without explicit approval. Review decision 2026-07-01: request_revision - User critique requires another Creep pass: restore the original smooth alien face and uninterrupted gray-blue skin while pushing the finish closer to Vellum-level matte gothic dry detail; current candidate still does not fully meet the Vellum matte/detail target.

review_history.reason: User critique requires another Creep pass: restore the original smooth alien face and uninterrupted gray-blue skin while pushing the finish closer to Vellum-level matte gothic dry detail; current candidate still does not fully meet the Vellum matte/detail target.

proofs.revision_request: User critique requires another Creep pass: restore the original smooth alien face and uninterrupted gray-blue skin while pushing the finish closer to Vellum-level matte gothic dry detail; current candidate still does not fully meet the Vellum matte/detail target.

proofs.proof_goal: Finalize Creep with smooth alien face/skin, dissociated horror identity, matte black scythe-ribbon silhouette, and Vellum-compatible dry gothic finish.
proofs.style_gate: Pass after user review: current favorite Creep, accepted as the live asset. It keeps smooth alien dissociation and matte black horror silhouette without corpse-gore drift, glossy creature skin, or shiny leather polish.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: objective standalone orange-fringe audit flagged 0 rows and the raw border sample was effectively exact safety-orange; board preview reads at 96 px.
proofs.decision_notes: Promoted live on 2026-07-02 after explicit user approval. Use as a narrow reference for Creep-like dissociated creepy humanoid/demon/cursed units. Do not let it demote Vellum as the primary material/detail/finish anchor.

proofs.proof_goal: Tank/weapon-heavy identity proof, first attempt.
proofs.style_gate: Fail: clean fantasy/cartoon metal drift; too heroic and too shiny for the matte gothic target.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: shield, hammer, and chain survived, but the raw style was rejected.
proofs.failure_reason: Preserved identity but lost matte goth non-shiny material language; armor read as clean fantasy/cartoon and too polished.
proofs.decision_notes: Kept as negative example. It created the hard matte gothic gate.

proofs.proof_goal: Correct tank/weapon-heavy proof after hard matte gate was added.
proofs.style_gate: Pass: grimier, darker, soot-stained, low-sheen tank with shield and hammer intact.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: checker/black/white review held the shield, hammer, and board-scale read.
proofs.decision_notes: Accepted as tank/weapon-heavy workflow proof. Not a live asset replacement.
proofs.prompt_context_notes: Quarantined by 2026-07-01 candidate triage after user critique: accepted as narrow tank/weapon-heavy history, but not safe as prompt/style context until fresh Vellum-first review clears the cartoony/less-matte concern.

proofs.proof_goal: Stress-test whether the hard matte gate can survive a bright blessed haloed titan without reverting to shiny paladin/chrome.
proofs.style_gate: Candidate pass: broad blessed titan identity, halo, crystals, chalky ivory stone, and tarnished gold survive with much less shine than the source.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: refined BiRefNet foreground-ML/despill cutout plus edge-orange-clean post-pass holds halo, shoulders, crystal spikes, hands, feet, and board-scale silhouette on checker/black/white review; orange-fringe audit now passes.
proofs.decision_notes: Current Korath candidate. It is useful as a haloed/divine tank stress proof, but the raw orange field is still a generated background rather than a mathematically flat key, so keep using BiRefNet and do not replace the live asset without human approval.

proofs.proof_goal: First-try stress-test whether the hard matte gate can preserve a bright support/caster identity without drifting into glossy anime, cheerful rave, or mobile-game magical-girl art.
proofs.style_gate: First-try candidate pass: adult moon-staff caster identity, turquoise hair, muted stained-glass dress panels, and dry low-sheen gothic material language survive without anime/magical-girl gloss.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: refined BiRefNet cutout preserves the crescent staff head, staff shaft, hair, extended hand, boots, and dress mass on checker/black/white review.
proofs.decision_notes: Current Luna candidate and first-try bright support/caster proof. It is not a live replacement. The raw orange field is generated rather than mathematically flat, so keep using BiRefNet and board-preview review.

proofs.proof_goal: Stress-test whether the hard matte gate can preserve a long diagonal polearm/scythe, cape, chains, and huge curved blade without cropping, tangling, or false mounted/horse drift.
proofs.style_gate: Candidate pass after one de-gloss iteration: standing red-black executioner identity, horned helmet, tattered cape, chains, full polearm, and huge blade survive with drier soot-black armor than attempt 1.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: refined BiRefNet cutout preserves the long polearm, large curved blade, helmet, cape, chains, feet, and 96 px board-scale read on checker/black/white review.
proofs.decision_notes: Current Morrak candidate. Attempt 1 solved the full-polearm composition but still had too much polished black armor; the selected attempt tightened dry soot-black material. Not a live replacement.

proofs.proof_goal: Stress-test whether the hard matte gate can preserve a thin contract/mogul silhouette, pale half-mask, small hand weapon, and grouped gold contract magic without glossy formalwear or board-scale collapse.
proofs.style_gate: Current candidate pass: thin Exile/Mogul identity, pale cracked half-mask, long matte coat, red lining, quill/ammo details, small hand weapon, and one grouped parchment-gold contract magic shape survive without glossy tuxedo drift.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: refined BiRefNet foreground-ML/despill cutout plus edge-orange-clean post-pass preserves the half-mask, coat tails, boots, small weapon, quills, and grouped magic shape; the board preview remains readable at 96 px on checker/black/white and orange-fringe audit now passes.
proofs.decision_notes: Current Teller candidate. The raw still has some realistic dark-cloth highlights, but it avoids shiny tuxedo/leather and keeps the small formal silhouette readable. Not a live replacement.

proofs.proof_goal: First-try stress-test whether the hard matte gate can hold on a massive red-black demon brute without wet monster skin, shiny lava cracks, comedy brute drift, or unreadable bulk.
proofs.style_gate: First-try current candidate: Bo keeps the huge horned executioner brute identity, tusked maw, red-black crack motif, bone spikes, clawed hands, and chained mace-head while reading as dry charcoal stone-hide and matte demon plates instead of wet skin or shiny lava.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: refined BiRefNet foreground-ML/despill cutout preserves the horns, broad torso, legs, clawed hand, chain, mace-head, and 96 px board read on checker/black/white.
proofs.decision_notes: Current Bo candidate and first-try proof for the large red-black demon brute risk. Not a live replacement.

proofs.proof_goal: First-try stress-test whether the hard matte gate can preserve a compact occult scholar/mentor silhouette without glossy feathers, cute owl drift, generic wizard drift, or board-scale collapse.
proofs.style_gate: First-try current candidate: Axiom keeps the severe owl face, cyan eyes, dark feather cloak mass, blue circular chest focus, talisman tags, clawed hands, taloned feet, and compact mentor silhouette without cute owl, generic wizard, or glossy-feather drift.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: refined BiRefNet foreground-ML/despill cutout preserves the feather cloak, owl head, robe, talismans, claws, taloned feet, and 96 px board read on checker/black/white.
proofs.decision_notes: Current Axiom candidate and first-try proof for the compact occult scholar/collector risk. Not a live replacement.

proofs.proof_goal: First-try stress-test whether the hard matte gate can preserve attached lightning/energy effects without neon superhero drift, detached particle-confetti, or cutout loss.
proofs.style_gate: First-try current candidate: Volt keeps the pale blue hair, crown-like spikes, cyan eyes, square chest orb, waist orb, dark blue robed stance, wraps, rope belt, and grouped hand lightning without neon superhero, robot, or particle-confetti drift.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: refined BiRefNet foreground-ML/despill cutout preserves the hand lightning, hair, crown spikes, chest/waist orbs, robe, wraps, and 96 px board read on checker/black/white.
proofs.decision_notes: Current Volt candidate and first-try proof for attached hand-lightning energy. Not a live replacement.

proofs.proof_goal: First-try stress-test whether the hard matte gate can preserve pale/sanguine brute flesh without wet gore, shiny anatomy, corpse-horror, or clean ogre drift.
proofs.style_gate: First-try current candidate: Vykos keeps the skull-maw face, red eyes, red crack marks, pale heavy torso, oversized clawed arms, bone spikes, dark loincloth, squat stance, and huge curved blade while reading as dry chalky bone/limestone flesh instead of wet gore or shiny anatomy.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: refined BiRefNet foreground-ML/despill cutout preserves the broad body, skull head, clawed arms, bone spikes, loincloth, huge curved blade, and 96 px board read on checker/black/white.
proofs.decision_notes: Current Vykos candidate and first-try proof for pale/sanguine flesh brute risk. Not a live replacement.

proofs.proof_goal: Stress-test whether the hard matte gate can preserve a huge stone-and-bone guardian tank without chrome armor, glossy wet rock, generic golem drift, or unreadable over-bulk.
proofs.style_gate: Current candidate after one rejected background pass: Brute keeps the skull face, small green eyes, rib armor, massive shoulder blocks, spiked cracked stone plates, slab fists, black tattered cloth, and tiny green fissures while reading as chalky dry stone, dusty bone, and dull oxidized iron instead of glossy wet rock, chrome armor, or a generic golem.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: refined BiRefNet foreground-ML/despill cutout was orange-cleaned by 84 saturated safety-orange pixels, preserving the skull, rib armor, shoulder spikes, slab fists, tattered cloth, feet, and 96 px board read on checker/black/white.
proofs.decision_notes: Current Brute candidate and proof for stone/bone guardian bulk. First raw pass was rejected because the orange background was too gradient; selected pass still required a tiny orange-clean step. Not a live replacement.

proofs.proof_goal: First-try stress-test whether the hard matte gate can preserve a small wiry raider with an oversized raised weapon without cute goblin, comedy mascot, sports-bat, polished cannon, or unreadable tiny-weapon drift.
proofs.style_gate: First-try current candidate: Bonko keeps the ivory grinning mask, pale eyes, wiry crouched body, long sinewy arms, spiked shoulder pad, ragged wraps, bare feet, and huge banded wooden bat/cannon-club held overhead while reading as dry scarred skin, dusty bone mask, splintered raw wood, dull iron bands, and frayed cloth instead of cute goblin, comedy mascot, sports bat, glossy plastic mask, or polished cannon.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: refined BiRefNet foreground-ML/despill cutout preserves the full raised weapon, mask, spiked shoulder pad, long limbs, ragged cloth, bare feet, and 96 px board read on checker/black/white.
proofs.decision_notes: Current Bonko candidate and first-try proof for small scavenger/imp weapon scale. Not a live replacement.

proofs.proof_goal: Stress-test whether the hard matte gate can preserve a prismatic time/blade executioner assassin without glossy black latex, glass armor, neon particle confetti, busy prism shards, or unreadable magical noise.
proofs.style_gate: Current candidate after one rejected glossy pass: Hexeon keeps the eye-covered crystal mask, small floating eye facets, spiked shoulders/back, clawed hands, thin predatory legs, wide assassin stance, and attached prismatic blade shards while reading as dry charcoal mineral, black chalk, matte gouache, dull smoky pigment, and chipped prismatic edges instead of glossy black latex, glass armor, neon confetti, or busy prism noise.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: refined BiRefNet foreground-ML/despill cutout plus edge-orange-clean post-pass preserves the head eyes, floating eye facets, shoulder/back spikes, claws, attached hand shards, thin legs, and 96 px board read on checker/black/white; orange-fringe audit now passes.
proofs.decision_notes: Current Hexeon candidate and proof for time/blade energy effects. First raw pass was rejected because the black body still read too glossy/latex-like. Not a live replacement.

proofs.proof_goal: First-try stress-test whether the hard matte gate can preserve a dry carved wood support idol without glossy varnished wood, toy totem, generic druid, green foliage blob, chrome armor, or unreadable bark fringe.
proofs.style_gate: Failing current candidate / audit negative control: Totem keeps the crown-like bark mask, cyan eyes, bark plates, turquoise markings, and readable guardian silhouette, but visual review says it still misses the Vellum target. The detail reads too clean-fantasy carved armor / stylized game-creature and not enough Vellum-level dry gothic fabric/parchment/ink richness, so matching the palette and having local detail is not enough.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: refined BiRefNet foreground-ML/despill cutout preserves the full crown mask, cyan eyes/runes, shoulder fringe, clawed hands/feet, lower bark tassels, and 96 px board read on checker/black/white.
proofs.decision_notes: Current Totem candidate is retained as a required style-audit negative control after user review. It must fail candidate style triage until revised; clean cutout and palette match do not make it a passing style proof. Not a live replacement.
proofs.revision_request: Revise Totem before any acceptance: keep the bark idol identity but move away from clean fantasy carved armor / stylized game-creature detail and toward Vellum-level dry gothic material richness, dirtier hand-painted surface breakup, heavier shadow, and less polished/heroic rendering.

style_audit_override.reason: user says Totem should fail: palette/detail matched but Vellum-level matte gothic finish did not

proofs.proof_goal: Stress-test whether the hard matte gate can preserve a grayscale spectral armored marksman with grouped tendril hair and ghost arms without smoke-only, cute ghost, werewolf, shiny armor, hair-spaghetti, or unreadable tendril-cloud drift.
proofs.style_gate: Current candidate after one rejected shiny/spaghetti pass: Sari keeps the grayscale spectral palette, green eyes, dark face mask/visor, soot-dull armor plates, leather straps, crouched leaping marksman silhouette, clawed hands, green rune marks, grouped wind-swept tendril hair, and ghostly extra arms/tendrils while avoiding cute ghost, smoke-only, werewolf, glossy latex, and unreadable tendril-cloud drift.
proofs.cutout_gate: Pass: refined BiRefNet foreground-ML/despill cutout preserves the green eye read, armored crouch, clawed hands, grouped hair/tendrils, trailing ghost arms, legs, and 96 px board read on checker/black/white.
proofs.decision_notes: Current Sari candidate and proof for monochrome spectral tendril units. First raw pass was rejected because the armor read too polished/shiny and hair was close to spaghetti noise. Not a live replacement.

proofs.proof_goal: Restore the source lunge, skull-pale grin, black eyes, gray alien/demon body, rear tendrils, and integrated scythe forearms while moving the material language toward Vellum-level dry matte gothic horror instead of wet creature anatomy.
proofs.style_gate: Current best research candidate: v6 preserves Berebell's low predator lunge, skull grin, black eyes, gray dry-hide read, occult scratches, and integrated scythe-arm silhouette better than v4/v5/v7. It is not accepted yet because minor wet-tendril and blade-edge residue still needs human review against Vellum.
proofs.cutout_gate: Not run in this pass; user asked to focus on artwork rather than cutout.
proofs.decision_notes: Sent Telegram sheet 455 and raw 456. v6 is the best-current artwork candidate, not a live replacement or accepted proof. v7 tightened some identity details but did not improve the material gate enough to replace v6.

It still needs human review for residual shoe/hair polish before any acceptance.
proofs.cutout_gate: Not run in this pass; user asked to focus on artwork rather than cutout.
proofs.decision_notes: Sent Telegram sheet 457 and raw 458. v2 improved face/cloth but used a portrait canvas; v3 restored square framing and is the best-current candidate. Not a live replacement.

It is still a review candidate because the demon torso remains more anatomical than Vellum's cloth/parchment anchor.

It is still a review candidate because beast units have less natural Vellum cloth/parchment material than humanoid anchors.

It remains a review candidate because the clean crystalline guardian type naturally carries less Vellum-like cloth/parchment grime and still needs human approval.

proofs.proof_goal: Revise Totem after user critique so the clean wooden guardian becomes a being marked by the evil folklore world: exhausted face, dead-root/famine wood, rope pressure, broken asymmetry, and cyan as scar pigment rather than shiny blue eyes/UI runes.
proofs.style_gate: Current best artwork candidate: v26 improves over the stale Totem negative control by adding exhausted face, dim cataract eyes, dead-root/famine wood, broken asymmetry, rope pressure, and cyan scar pigment while preserving the guardian silhouette. It remains a review candidate because it is denser than the source and not a cutout-grade raw.

proofs.decision_notes: Previously sent Telegram raw 452 and sheet 453. Keep the earlier totem_dry_wood_guardian_refit row as a style-audit negative control; v26 supersedes it as best-current research candidate. Not a live replacement.

proofs.proof_goal: Keep Creep's original smooth alien face/skin and circular black blade-tendril silhouette while avoiding corpse anatomy, ripped flesh, wet creature gloss, and low-detail smoothness.
proofs.style_gate: Accepted after user review on 2026-07-02: v5 keeps the smooth alien identity and reduces wet/corpse drift better than later v8, which added ink-ribbon drama but still failed the Vellum lock. It is the live Creep direction, while Vellum remains the global material/detail anchor.
proofs.cutout_gate: Existing refined BiRefNet foreground-ML/despill/edge-clean cutout and board preview exist from the v5 workflow.
proofs.decision_notes: Sent Telegram sheet 466 and raw 467 in this pass. A new v9 generation was attempted twice on 2026-07-02 but the image service returned unrelated infographic images, so those outputs were discarded and not copied into the project. Later on 2026-07-02, the user explicitly approved/favorited Creep v5 and it was copied to assets/units/creep.png. See accepted proof row creep_smooth_alien_matte_v5_live.

It is not a full creative regeneration and should not be treated as an accepted proof.

coverage_gaps.risk: later unit proofs can match the darker palette and cutout workflow while losing the high-detail dry gothic illustration quality of Vellum and Paisley
small non-unit assets are only proven by one contract-mark token so far; metal, glass, cloth, and icon silhouettes may still drift glossy or unreadable

narrow white-silver aegis guardians with gems or crystalline armor can drift into chrome robots, angels, anime mechs, glossy latex bodies, or unreadable pale silhouettes instead of dull pearl/bone matte guardians
individual proof wins do not yet prove that every remaining live sprite or future unit can be recreated first-try from the matrix without manual prompt surgery

next_recommended_stress_test.reason: Revise Creep before Veyra or broader roster work; user wants smooth alien identity restored and Vellum-level matte gothic detail locked harder.
